title: Mildly Pungent Syrian Paneer Curry
description: A creamy and pungent Syrian dish featuring paneer, this curry is a crowd-pleaser and FODMAP-friendly, made by boiling the ingredients in a flavorful broth.
instructions: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat and saute the cumin seeds until fragrant.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to brown.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
Add the coriander powder, turmeric powder, and red chili powder, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the paneer cubes and cook until they are lightly browned on all sides.
Add the coconut milk, salt, and water, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the curry for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the curry hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
